questions.question: What is GitHub & Collaboration and why is it important?
questions.answer: GitHub & Collaboration is a fundamental concept that Repositories, pull requests, code review, and GitHub workflows. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding GitHub & Collaboration allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply GitHub & Collaboration in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, GitHub & Collaboration is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with GitHub & Collaboration?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain GitHub & Collaboration to a beginner?
questions.answer: GitHub & Collaboration is essentially about repositories, pull requests, code review, and github workflows. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
